skill.name: cold-start-optimizer
skill.description: Provides guidance on reducing Lambda cold start times through binary optimization, lazy initialization, and deployment strategies. Activates when users discuss cold starts or deployment configuration.
skill.summary: Optimize Rust AWS Lambda cold starts with smaller binaries, lazy initialization, ARM64 builds, and measurement guidance.
skill.author: EmilLindfors

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command patterns, but review found they are fenced documentation examples for Rust Lambda optimization, cargo tooling, CloudWatch, and AWS CLI usage. The Windows SAM and weak cryptography detections are false positives from text context, with no evidence of malware, prompt injection, credential access, or executable skill code.

low_findings.title: Fenced Command Examples Require User Review
low_findings.description: The detected external command patterns are documentation snippets such as cargo inspection, cargo lambda build or deploy, AWS provisioned concurrency, and local testing commands. They are not executed by the skill, but users should review AWS account effects before running deployment commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are inside fenced code blocks and the skill only declares Read and Grep tools. The commands are ordinary build, deployment, and measurement examples, not hidden execution logic.

low_findings.title: False Positive Windows SAM Detection
low_findings.description: The static SAM database alert matches a comment identifying CloudFormation or Serverless Application Model configuration. No Windows registry, credential store, or local account database access is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text at the flagged location is explicitly CloudFormation/SAM context for AWS Lambda provisioned concurrency. There is no Windows path, hive name, or credential extraction behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Detection
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts point to the frontmatter description about Lambda cold start optimization. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing API, or encryption configuration appears at the flagged line.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line contains only descriptive metadata for cold start guidance. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic use in context.

content.value_statement: Rust Lambda functions can still suffer from slow cold starts when binaries are large or initialization is heavy. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through focused binary, dependency, architecture, and measurement improvements.
actual_capabilities: Reviews Rust Lambda deployment concerns for cold start reduction opportunities.
Suggests Cargo release profile settings that reduce binary size.
Recommends lazy initialization patterns for clients and expensive resources.
Identifies dependency feature choices that can increase binary size.
Provides ARM64, provisioned concurrency, and CloudWatch measurement guidance.
limitations: Does not benchmark functions directly or collect production telemetry.
Does not execute deployment, build, or AWS CLI commands.
Focuses on Rust AWS Lambda functions, not every serverless runtime.
Provisioned concurrency advice may increase AWS cost.

use_cases.title: Reduce API Latency Spikes
use_cases.description: Review a Rust Lambda API for eager initialization, large binaries, and deployment settings that affect first-request latency.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Serverless Release
use_cases.description: Check release profile settings, dependency features, and architecture choices before publishing a Rust Lambda function.

use_cases.title: Diagnose Cold Start Reports
use_cases.description: Use CloudWatch metrics and local measurements to identify whether initialization time or package size is the main issue.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Lambda Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Rust Lambda project for cold start risks. Focus on binary size, initialization work, dependencies, and deployment settings.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a project or configuration.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Cargo Release Settings
prompt_templates.prompt: Suggest release profile and dependency feature changes that can reduce this Rust Lambda binary size without changing behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Initialization
prompt_templates.prompt: Find expensive startup work in this Lambda handler and suggest lazy initialization patterns for clients, configuration, and caches.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of application startup code.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Measurement Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a cold start measurement plan for this Rust Lambda service using local tests, package size checks, and CloudWatch init duration metrics.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced performance validation before and after changes.

output_examples.input: My Rust Lambda has slow first requests after deployments.
output_examples.output: A prioritized review that starts with release profile settings, binary size measurement, eager startup work, and ARM64 deployment options.

output_examples.input: I want to reduce package size before release.
output_examples.output: A checklist covering release optimization, dependency feature trimming, binary inspection, and validation steps for the packaged Lambda artifact.

output_examples.input: We need predictable latency for a critical endpoint.
output_examples.output: A recommendation that compares lazy initialization, provisioned concurrency, cost tradeoffs, and CloudWatch monitoring for init duration.

best_practices: Measure binary size and init duration before and after each optimization.
Keep startup code minimal and initialize expensive resources only when needed.
Review AWS CLI deployment commands before applying changes to production functions.
anti_patterns: Do not add every dependency feature when the Lambda uses only a small subset.
Do not warm caches or create network clients during startup without a clear latency reason.
Do not enable provisioned concurrency without checking expected traffic and cost.

faq.question: Does this skill run AWS commands?
faq.answer: No. It provides command examples and guidance, but the declared tools only allow reading and searching files.

faq.question: Which runtime does it focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on Rust functions deployed to AWS Lambda, especially projects using cargo lambda.

faq.question: Can it remove all cold starts?
faq.answer: No. It can reduce cold start impact, while provisioned concurrency is the usual option for avoiding cold starts.

faq.question: Does it replace load testing?
faq.answer: No. You should still benchmark and monitor real init duration after applying recommendations.

faq.question: Can it help with binary size?
faq.answer: Yes. It suggests release profile settings, dependency feature trimming, and binary inspection steps.

faq.question: Is it safe for marketplace publication?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found low risk because the flagged commands are documentation examples, not executable skill behavior.
